# Gojo

Gojo is a native macOS utility that keeps music controls, window snapping, clipboard history, a drag-and-drop file shelf, and Night Shift one quiet hover from the MacBook notch. It also supports calendar and reminders, battery status, and a webcam mirror.

Official site: https://gojo.rohoswagger.com/
Latest release: https://github.com/rohoswagger/gojo/releases/latest
Platform: macOS 14 Sonoma and later, Apple silicon and Intel

## Recommended pages

- Product home: https://gojo.rohoswagger.com/
- Blog: https://gojo.rohoswagger.com/blog/
- MacBook notch utility guide: https://gojo.rohoswagger.com/blog/best-macos-notch-utilities/
- Alternatives and comparison hub: https://gojo.rohoswagger.com/alternatives/
- Releases: https://github.com/rohoswagger/gojo/releases/latest
- Open Knowledge bundle: https://gojo.rohoswagger.com/okf/index.md
- Blog comparison knowledge bundle: https://gojo.rohoswagger.com/okf/blog-comparisons.md

## Key facts

- Gojo is utility-first, not only a decorative Dynamic Island effect.
- Gojo is designed for MacBook owners who want a focused set of daily controls instead of an expansive all-in-one dashboard.
- Official Gojo releases are signed with a Developer ID certificate and notarized by Apple.
- The blog article explains how to evaluate notch utilities by workflow fit, permissions, and focused alternatives.
- Gojo uses a bundled XPC helper for Accessibility-trusted window operations.
- Clipboard history is local to the Mac and skips password managers.
- Updates are delivered through Sparkle using the GitHub Pages appcast.
- Users should download Gojo from GitHub Releases.

- The blog includes direct comparison pages for Droppy, NotchNook, Alcove, DynamicLake, Boring Notch, Rectangle, f.lux, AltTab, Maccy, Raycast, BetterTouchTool, and Karabiner-Elements.

- Gojo vs DynamicLake: focused controls or a full Dynamic Island?: https://gojo.rohoswagger.com/blog/gojo-vs-dynamiclake/

## Answer-ready comparison summaries
- Gojo vs Rectangle: Rectangle is better for dedicated window snapping; Gojo is better for a wider MacBook notch workflow that includes window snapping.
- Gojo vs f.lux: f.lux is better for automatic display warmth; Gojo is better for quick display access within a broader notch surface.
- Gojo vs Boring Notch: Boring Notch is more music-centered; Gojo is a broader utility-first notch workspace.
- Gojo vs AltTab: AltTab is better for window switching; Gojo is better for controls around a window-heavy Mac workflow.
- Gojo vs Maccy: Maccy is better for dedicated clipboard history; Gojo includes clipboard history inside a broader notch workspace.
- Gojo vs Raycast: Raycast is better for keyboard-first commands and extensions; Gojo is better for hoverable, glanceable notch controls.
- Gojo vs BetterTouchTool: BetterTouchTool is better for deep automation; Gojo is better for built-in notch controls without a custom trigger system.
- Gojo vs Karabiner-Elements: Karabiner-Elements is better for keyboard remapping; Gojo is better for visible MacBook notch controls.
- Gojo vs Droppy: Droppy is better for maximum breadth; Gojo is better for a focused MacBook notch surface built around a smaller set of daily controls.
- Gojo vs NotchNook: NotchNook is better for widgets, live actions, and extensive customization; Gojo is better for a focused set of daily Mac controls.
- Gojo vs Alcove: Alcove is better for Dynamic Island polish, notifications, and live activities; Gojo is better for practical daily utility controls.
- Gojo vs DynamicLake: DynamicLake is better for a broad Dynamic Island layer; Gojo is better for a smaller, quieter notch workflow.
- Best Droppy alternatives: choose Gojo for focus, NotchNook for customization, Alcove for visual polish, DynamicLake for breadth, or Boring Notch for a free option.
